abstract: Skeletal editing has emerged as a powerful synthetic paradigm for the precise atom-by-atom modification of core ring systems, offering a level of structural diversification that traditional peripheral functionalization cannot achieve. While recent advances in arene skeletal editing have leveraged carbene- or nitrene-type insertions and complex rearrangements, these methodologies have largely remained inapplicable to the pyridine nucleus. Here, we present a platform for the direct skeletal editing of pyridines via a formal CN-to-CC atom-pair swap, enabling the modular synthesis of substituted benzenes and naphthalenes. This transformation is achieved through a streamlined, one-pot sequence involving dearomatization, [4+2] cycloaddition, and rearomatizing retro-cycloaddition. By selecting specific cycloaddition partners, we can direct the installation of diverse substituents at precisely defined positions on the newly formed carbocyclic core. The utility and robustness of this approach are further underscored by the late-stage skeletal remodeling of several complex, pyridine-containing pharmaceuticals.
